title: Loading & Preprocessing Images
concept: Resizing images and normalizing pixel values to match the CV backbone's input requirements.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of an object detection project?

options.text: To measure the brightness of images
To detect specific objects and highlight them

exercises.explain: The main goal of an object detection project is to detect specific objects and highlight them using bounding boxes.

cards.title: Welcome to Image Preprocessing!
cards.body: In this lesson, we will learn how to prepare images for our object detection project by resizing and normalizing them.

cards.title: What is Resizing?
cards.body: Resizing is changing the dimensions of an image so it fits a particular size required by the system we’re working with.

cards.title: Think of It Like a Photo Frame!
cards.body: Resizing an image is like choosing a photo frame for a picture. You need the right size so that it fits perfectly!

cards.title: Example of Resizing
cards.body: If we have an image that is 400x300 pixels but our model needs 224x224 pixels, we must resize it down to that size.

cards.title: Myth: Bigger Images Are Always Better
cards.body: Some believe that bigger images always give better results. Actually, models need images of specific sizes!

cards.title: Image Before and After Resizing
cards.body: See how the image size changes, but the content remains visible!

exercises.prompt: What does resizing an image accomplish?

options.text: It changes the color of the image
It alters the image’s dimensions to fit the model
It adds more details to the image
It removes objects from the image

exercises.explain: Resizing an image alters its dimensions to fit the specific input size required by the model.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Resizing makes an image more beautiful.
exercises.explain: Resizing is about fitting the image to model requirements, not about making it look better.

exercises.prompt: When preparing images for modeling, we often need to resize them to a specific ____.
exercises.sentence: When preparing images for modeling, we often need to resize them to a specific size.
exercises.explain: We resize images to a specific size required by the model.

exercises.prompt: Which option involves changing the pixel values of an image?
exercises.explain: Normalizing the pixel values adjusts the image data so the model can work with it more effectively.

exercises.prompt: What are two key steps to prepare images for an object detection model?
exercises.explain: The key steps of resizing the images to fit the model input size and normalizing pixel values ensure that the images are processed correctly for effective detection.
